document: So the idea is basically a small patch you stick on your skin — like a nicotine patch or a continuous glucose monitor, but without any needle or filament going under the skin. It would shine some kind of light through the skin, maybe infrared or something like that, and pick up on how glucose in the blood absorbs or reflects that light differently depending on concentration. The patch reads that signal continuously and sends it to your phone. Diabetics could just wear it and always know their blood sugar without ever pricking their finger.
